name: Broiled Sesame Trout
recipeIngredient: Or other locally available trout, pan-dressed
recipeInstructions:
1. With a sharp knife, make three light slashes on each side of the fish, without cutting the flesh too deep.
2. In a 13" by 9" baking pan, mix lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Add the fish and turn over to coat with marinade. Cover; refrigerate at least 3 hours, turning occasionally.
3. In a 1-quart saucepan over medium heat, toast sesame seeds until golden, stirring and shaking the pan occasionally. Add butter or margarine and heat until melted.
4. Place the fish on a rack in a broiling pan. Drain marinade from the baking pan into the sesame seed mixture. Preheat the broiler according to manufacturer directions.
5. Broil the fish about 5 minutes on each side, basting frequently with the sesame seed mixture.
6. Test the fish for doneness with a fork. Trout is cooked when its flesh flakes easily.
7. With pancake turners or spatulas, lift the fish carefully onto a warm platter and spoon hot juices over it.
8. Carve the top side of the fish into servings, just down to the bone. Carefully ease the cooked meat away from the rib bones; place one section on a warmed plate. Repeat with remaining sections.
9. Lift off all bones and carve the lower section in the same way (if the fish is stuffed, remove stuffing after cutting the top of the fish and before removing bones to serve the lower portion).
